title: rtransparent enhancement roadmap
text: Living plan improving package. Update items ship.

title: rtransparent enhancement roadmap
text: Improve accuracy iteratively, one PR per improvement, merged master. Measure every detection change benchmark (Rscript data-raw/benchmark/run_all.R); merge metrics improve sensitivity regress. Accuracy coverage. Keep name rtransparent now (revisit rename release). self-contained: reimplement oddpub’s data/code detection natively (GPL-3), vendor oddpub (AGPL-3). Fetch article XML NCBI PubMed Central (EFetch + OAI, R/rt_fetch.R), Europe PMC (different corpus, incompatible JATS flavor).

title: rtransparent enhancement roadmap
text: #1 v0.3.0 baseline: novelty/replication detectors, registry expansion (ISRCTN/ANZCTR/DRKS/IRCT/UMIN), testthat suite. #2 oddpub/tokenizers made optional (Suggests + guards); NCBI PMC fetch helper R/rt_fetch.R. #3 accuracy benchmark (R/benchmark.R, data-raw/benchmark/, inst/benchmark/); fixed .reroot_xml (returned empty non-OAI XML) unqualified str_detect funding detector. #4 funding specificity 78% → 96% tightening get_fund_acknow_new.

what: A. Port quest-bih fork fixes [done / assessed]
title: rtransparent enhancement roadmap
text: quest-bih/rtransparent ahead 7 commits serghiou. - prospero detection fix — ported (PROSPERO id now 5 11 digits, TXT + PMC). benchmark delta (held-set PROSPERO-cases). - coi update (TXT rt_coi, 75 lines) — deferred: TXT path, exercised PMC benchmark; revisit TXT benchmark added. - register pipe update (808 + 686 line reformat register/xml_utils/utils) — skipped: cosmetic, conflicts heavily line’s divergence. - “harmonize new oddpub” — moot reimplement data/code (item B).

what: B. Reimplement oddpub data/code detection natively (GPL-3) [done, tuning recall]
title: rtransparent enhancement roadmap
text: Native detector R/data_code.R (.detect_data_code): field-specific accession schemes, repository URLs/names, deposit/availability + DAS language, supplement file-format signals, reuse non-availability vetoes. rt_data_code / rt_data_code_pmc / rt_data_code_pmc_list now use longer need oddpub/tokenizers runtime. Benchmark (data-raw/benchmark/run_data_code.R, inst/benchmark/results_data_code.md): - code 68% sens / 94% spec (beats paper’s ~59% sensitivity). - data 64% sens / 95% spec (precision matches oddpub’s ~97%; recall oddpub’s ~84% long tail supplement-data rare phrasings). Remaining: lift data recall past oddpub (extend supplement/file-format DAS phrasings; ~5 misses extraction gaps, ~21 pattern-tail, ~15 detectable text signal). delete now-dead oddpub/tokenizers helper functions rt_data_code_pmc.R drop Suggests.

title: rtransparent enhancement roadmap
text: exported rt_fund_pmc broken: predicts TRUE essentially input empty text. rt_all_pmc use (uses internal .get_fund_pmc + .rt_fund_pmc). Align public function working internal path, add test public API correct.

title: rtransparent enhancement roadmap
text: COI sensitivity (94%) data-limited, detector defect. 5 held-false negatives COI statement anywhere XML (COI PubMed/PDF ; paper’s documented limitation). detector catches 100% COI actually XML. Recovering needs PubMed CoiStatement lookup (feature, detector fix). Funding residual false positives (5/116 negatives). 3 <funding-statement> naming institution (definitional, risk false negatives “fixed”); 1 “Financial source: none” (negation reaches prediction path current negation steps miss); 1 author-contributions leak. Low value; defer. CRAN-readiness pass (Rd/examples/vignette deps, R CMD check) release; decide possible rename (rtransparentplus?) point.

title: Introduction to rtransparent
text: rtransparent identifies extracts indicators transparency full text published biomedical articles. works two inputs: plain TXT files (typically converted PDFs) PMC XML files (JATS XML served PubMed Central). indicator returns whether indicator found , found, sentence statement triggered detection. rt_all rt_all_pmc run COI, funding, registration, novelty replication detectors together single pass. Data code sharing exposed separately rt_data_code / rt_data_code_pmc. package validation described Serghiou et al., Assessment transparency indicators across biomedical literature: open open? (PLOS Biology, 2021, doi:10.1371/journal.pbio.3001107).

title: Introduction to rtransparent
text: PMC XML parsed xml2. XML root standardized <article> node (package accepts OAI-PMH, EFetch <pmc-articleset> bare <article> shapes), namespace optionally stripped (remove_ns = TRUE), text split sections indicator usually appears: acknowledgments, footnotes / author notes, body, methods, abstract supplementary material. TXT files read whole split paragraphs.

title: Introduction to rtransparent
text: Detection rule-based interpretable: indicator curated set regular expressions applied relevant sections, rather machine learning model. keeps output auditable (matched statement returned) reproducible. Conflicts interest. Detected structured COI footnotes (fn-type = "conflict"), section titles (“Conflicts interest”, “Competing interests”, “Declaration interest”, “Duality interest”), set text patterns covering financial relationships, consulting, fees, board membership, patents explicit “competing interests” declarations. Honoraria--subjects reference text masked reduce false positives. Funding. Detected XML <funding-group> element, funding section titles, text patterns “supported ”, “funded ”, “grant / number”, named funders award types. Acknowledged funding required use explicit funding language (funding verb tied funder), bare mention institution word “support” enough. -funding declarations excluded. Protocol registration. Detected registry identifiers (ClinicalTrials.gov NCT, PROSPERO CRD, ISRCTN, ANZCTR ACTRN, DRKS, IRCT, UMIN, ChiCTR) registration phrasing methods footnotes. Novelty replication. Detected claim patterns “first time”, “knowledge”, “novel finding” (novelty) “replicate”, “independently validated”, “confirmatory cohort” (replication), negation filters (“failed replicate”). Data code sharing. Detected native detector (.detect_data_code) built public facts validated statements: field-specific accession schemes (GEO GSE, SRA / BioProject PRJNA, PDB, ArrayExpress, dbGaP, ProteomeXchange, Dryad / Zenodo / figshare DOIs, …), repository URLs names, deposit / availability / data-availability-statement language, supplement file-format signals. Crucially distinguishes sharing (“data deposited GEO”) reuse (“data downloaded GEO”) excludes “available request”. Code repositories (GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket) count data paired data noun, code-GitHub link mistaken data sharing.

title: Summarizing transparency across a corpus
text: Running detector single article returns one-row table indicators: study corpus run detector many files stack rows (example rt_data_code_pmc_list(), purrr::map_dfr(files, rt_all_pmc)). result one row per article indicator columns is_coi_pred, is_fund_pred, is_register_pred, is_open_data, is_open_code (is_novelty_pred, is_replication_pred). package ships small simulated table shape, rt_demo, rest vignette runs without downloading anything:
code: library(rtransparent) #> rtransparent 0.5.0: identify indicators of transparency (conflicts of interest, funding, #> protocol registration, novelty, replication, and data and code sharing) in #> biomedical articles. GitHub: https://github.com/choxos/rtransparent | vignette("rtransparent")  xml <- system.file(   "extdata", "PMID32171256-PMC7071725.xml", package = "rtransparent" ) one <- rt_all_pmc(xml, remove_ns = TRUE) one[, c("pmid", "is_coi_pred", "is_fund_pred", "is_register_pred")] #> # A tibble: 1 × 4 #>   pmid     is_coi_pred is_fund_pred is_register_pred #>   <chr>    <lgl>       <lgl>        <lgl>            #> 1 32171256 TRUE        TRUE         FALSE data(rt_demo) head(rt_demo) #> # A tibble: 6 × 10 #>   pmid      year type     is_coi_pred is_fund_pred is_register_pred is_open_data #>   <chr>    <int> <chr>    <lgl>       <lgl>        <lgl>            <lgl>        #> 1 38281623  2011 researc… FALSE       TRUE         FALSE            FALSE        #> 2 35191245  2014 researc… TRUE        FALSE        FALSE            FALSE        #> 3 37613201  2022 researc… TRUE        TRUE         FALSE            TRUE         #> 4 27960187  2021 researc… FALSE       FALSE        FALSE            FALSE        #> 5 27740712  2024 researc… TRUE        TRUE         TRUE             FALSE        #> 6 26032088  2010 review-… FALSE       FALSE        FALSE            FALSE        #> # ℹ 3 more variables: is_open_code <lgl>, is_novelty_pred <lgl>, #> #   is_replication_pred <lgl>

title: Summarizing transparency across a corpus
text: rt_summary() reports, indicator, many articles assessed, many positive, apparent prevalence 95% confidence interval:
code: s <- rt_summary(rt_demo) knitr::kable(   s[, c("label", "n_articles", "n_detected", "percent", "conf_low", "conf_high")],   digits = 1,   col.names = c("Indicator", "Assessed", "Detected", "%", "CI low", "CI high") )

title: Summarizing transparency across a corpus
text: text-mining detector perfect, observed prevalence biased estimate true prevalence. rt_summary() corrects using detector’s validated sensitivity specificity (Rogan-Gladen estimator). correction default adds adj_percent, adj_low adj_high: accuracy values come rt_accuracy: Novelty replication validated accuracy , corrected values NA. use validation (published oddpub values data code), pass table variable, sensitivity specificity columns:
code: knitr::kable(   s[, c("label", "percent", "adj_percent", "adj_low", "adj_high")],   digits = 1,   col.names = c("Indicator", "Apparent %", "Corrected %", "CI low", "CI high") ) rt_accuracy #> # A tibble: 5 × 5 #>   variable         label                 sensitivity specificity source          #>   <chr>            <chr>                       <dbl>       <dbl> <chr>           #> 1 is_coi_pred      Conflicts of interest       0.992       0.995 Serghiou et al… #> 2 is_fund_pred     Funding disclosure          0.997       0.981 Serghiou et al… #> 3 is_register_pred Protocol registration       0.955       0.997 Serghiou et al… #> 4 is_open_data     Data sharing                0.643       0.95  rtransparent n… #> 5 is_open_code     Code sharing                0.679       0.94  rtransparent n… my_acc <- rt_accuracy my_acc$sensitivity[my_acc$variable == "is_open_data"] <- 0.758 rt_summary(rt_demo, indicators = "is_open_data", accuracy = my_acc)[,   c("label", "percent", "adj_percent")] #> # A tibble: 1 × 3 #>   label        percent adj_percent #>   <chr>          <dbl>       <dbl> #> 1 Data sharing    16.8        16.6

title: Summarizing transparency across a corpus
text: rt_score() adds per-article count openness practices met (conflicts interest, funding, registration, data code). Tabulating shows many articles meet zero, one, two … five practices:
code: scored <- rt_score(rt_demo) knitr::kable(   as.data.frame(table(`Practices met` = scored$n_indicators)),   col.names = c("Practices met", "Articles") )

title: Summarizing transparency across a corpus
text: Pass summarize within grouping column, article type:
code: by_type <- rt_summary(rt_demo, by = "type", adjust = FALSE) knitr::kable(   by_type[by_type$indicator == "is_open_data",           c("type", "label", "n_articles", "percent")],   digits = 1,   col.names = c("Type", "Indicator", "Assessed", "%") )

title: Summarizing transparency across a corpus
text: rt_plot() returns ggplot, composes usual ggplot2 layers. default prevalence bar chart:  Use type = "trend" year column see prevalence time:  Set adjusted = TRUE either plot show error-corrected prevalence instead apparent prevalence.
code: library(ggplot2) rt_plot(rt_demo) + ggtitle("Transparency indicators in rt_demo") rt_plot(rt_demo, type = "trend", year = "year")

title: Summarizing transparency across a corpus
text: typical analysis therefore: run detector corpus, stack rows, per-indicator detection methodology, see vignette("rtransparent").
code: results <- purrr::map_dfr(xml_files, rt_all_pmc, remove_ns = TRUE) rt_summary(results)                       # prevalence + corrected prevalence rt_score(results)                         # per-article practice count rt_plot(results, type = "trend", year = "year")

title: Identifies Indicators of Transparency
text: rtransparent R package automatically identifies extracts indicators transparency full text published biomedical articles, TXT (PDF-derived) PMC XML form. indicator returns whether indicator found , found, statement triggered detection. detects seven indicators: Conflicts interest (rt_coi, rt_coi_pmc) Funding (rt_fund, rt_fund_pmc) Protocol registration (rt_register, rt_register_pmc) Novelty claims (rt_novelty, rt_novelty_pmc) Replication components (rt_replication, rt_replication_pmc) Data sharing code sharing (rt_data_code, rt_data_code_pmc) rt_all / rt_all_pmc run COI, funding, registration, novelty replication together; rt_meta_pmc returns article metadata PMC XML file. Detection rule-based interpretable (curated regular expressions relevant article sections), output auditable reproducible. See vignette (vignette("rtransparent")) methodology, package website https://choxos.github.io/rtransparent full documentation.

title: Identifies Indicators of Transparency
text: Original package Stylianos (Stelios) Serghiou. enhanced fork maintained Ahmad Sofi-Mahmudi (ORCID 0000-0001-6829-0823, GitHub @choxos). original data code detection relied oddpub package Nico Riedel; data code detection now implemented natively longer requires oddpub.

title: Identifies Indicators of Transparency
text: Naming convention: functions operate TXT files end _pmc; functions operate PMC XML end _pmc. best way learn package vignette: vignette("rtransparent").
code: library(rtransparent)  # A bundled example PMC XML file xml <- system.file("extdata", "PMID32171256-PMC7071725.xml", package = "rtransparent")  # COI, funding, registration, novelty and replication in one pass rt_all_pmc(xml, remove_ns = TRUE)  # Data and code sharing rt_data_code_pmc(xml, remove_ns = TRUE)  # Article metadata rt_meta_pmc(xml, remove_ns = TRUE)

title: Identifies Indicators of Transparency
text: detectors describe one article time. study whole corpus, stack per-article rows summarize : accuracy correction uses bundled rt_accuracy table (validated detector sensitivity specificity), can override. See vignette("transparency-summary") full walk-.
code: # results: one row per article (e.g. purrr::map_dfr(files, rt_all_pmc)) data(rt_demo)            # a small simulated example shipped with the package  rt_summary(rt_demo)      # prevalence of each indicator, with a confidence                          # interval and a sensitivity/specificity-corrected                          # (Rogan-Gladen) prevalence  rt_score(rt_demo)        # add a per-article count of openness practices met  rt_plot(rt_demo)                              # prevalence bar chart rt_plot(rt_demo, type = "trend", year = "year")  # prevalence over time

title: Identifies Indicators of Transparency
text: Benchmarked human-labeled gold standard Serghiou et al. (2021) held-XML test set (reproducible data-raw/benchmark/, results inst/benchmark/): native code detector exceeds paper’s reported sensitivity data detector’s precision matches original oddpub. native data sensitivity (64%) currently oddpub’s ~84% set, tail supplement-data rare phrasings, improved; treat native data detector high-precision yet complete sensitivity replacement.

title: Summarize transparency indicators across a corpus of articles — rt_summary
text: Takes data frame one row per article (output [rt_all_pmc()] joined [rt_data_code_pmc()], stacked many articles) returns prevalence transparency indicator. indicator reports number articles assessed, number indicator detected, apparent prevalence Wilson confidence interval , optionally, prevalence corrected detector's sensitivity specificity (Rogan-Gladen estimator).

title: Summarize transparency indicators across a corpus of articles — rt_summary
code: rt_summary(   data,   indicators = NULL,   by = NULL,   adjust = TRUE,   accuracy = NULL,   conf_level = 0.95 )

title: Summarize transparency indicators across a corpus of articles — rt_summary
text: data data frame one row per article. Indicator columns logical (0/1) named [rt_all_pmc()]: `is_coi_pred`, `is_fund_pred`, `is_register_pred`, `is_open_data`, `is_open_code`, `is_novelty_pred`, `is_replication_pred`. `NA` marks article assessed indicator excluded denominator. indicators Optional character vector indicator columns summarize. Defaults every recognized indicator present `data`. Optional name grouping column (example publication year, journal article type); summary computed within group. adjust `TRUE` (default), add prevalence corrected detector sensitivity specificity using `accuracy`. Indicators absent `accuracy` receive `NA` corrected values. accuracy data frame detector accuracy columns `variable`, `sensitivity` `specificity`. Defaults [rt_accuracy]. conf_level Confidence level intervals (default `0.95`).

title: Summarize transparency indicators across a corpus of articles — rt_summary
text: tibble one row per indicator (per group, `` given):   grouping column (`` used), `indicator`, `label`,   `n_articles`, `n_detected`, `percent`, `conf_low`, `conf_high` ,   `adjust = TRUE`, `adj_percent`, `adj_low` `adj_high`. Percentages   interval bounds 0-100 scale.

title: rtransparent 0.5.0
text: rt_summary() reports indicator’s prevalence Wilson confidence interval , default, prevalence corrected detector’s sensitivity specificity (Rogan-Gladen estimator). can summarize within groups via . rt_score() adds per-article count openness practices met. rt_plot() draws prevalence bar chart prevalence--time line chart (requires ggplot2). New datasets: rt_accuracy (validated detector sensitivity specificity, used rt_summary()) rt_demo (small simulated corpus examples). New vignette, vignette("transparency-summary"), illustrating output: one article corpus prevalence table, accuracy-corrected prevalence, practice-count distribution, subgroup summaries plots.

title: rtransparent 0.4.1
text: Fixed exported rt_fund_pmc(). previously predicted funding TRUE -funding articles empty evidence text; now delegates detection path rt_all_pmc() two agree, positive prediction always carries evidence. Added regression tests. Exported documented rt_meta_pmc() (article metadata PMC XML file), README advertised exported. Generated missing help pages novelty replication functions. R CMD check now passes errors warnings. Rewrote vignette self-contained (runs bundled example XML, PDF download steps shown executed) describe package methodology; removed stale oddpub / metareadr instructions. Updated README benchmark documentation native data code detection, pointed URLs maintained fork, regenerated committed benchmark results current version. Added package startup message removed stale packrat configuration.

title: rtransparent 0.4.0
text: Data code sharing detection now implemented natively (R/data_code.R) longer requires oddpub package runtime. held-validation set native detector scores data 64% sensitivity / 95% specificity code 68% sensitivity / 94% specificity (published paper reports 76% 59% sensitivity). Code detection already exceeds paper’s sensitivity data precision matches original oddpub; data sensitivity improved toward oddpub’s ~84%. rt_data_code, rt_data_code_pmc rt_data_code_pmc_list rewritten use native detector return is_open_data / is_open_code matched statement text. longer depend oddpub tokenizers. Added data/code benchmark (data-raw/benchmark/run_data_code.R, inst/benchmark/results_data_code.md).

title: rtransparent 0.3.4
text: Ported PROSPERO detection fix quest-bih fork: registration regex now matches PROSPERO identifiers 5 11 digits (CRD numbers exceed 5 digits), TXT PMC detectors. change benchmark (held-set PROSPERO-cases). fork’s commits assessed deferred: “coi update” TXT-(exercised PMC benchmark) “pipe update” cosmetic reformat conflicts line’s changes.

title: rtransparent 0.3.3
text: Improved funding specificity 78% 96% held-XML test set (accuracy 86% 97%) tightening get_fund_acknow_new(). previously flagged acknowledgment merely named institution used word “support”, competing-interest statements, generic thanks, data-availability statements affiliations misread funding. now requires explicit funding language: funding verb directed funder, institutional “support/funding …”, grant award identifier, named award. Sensitivity unchanged 100% test set.

title: rtransparent 0.3.2
text: Added reproducible accuracy benchmark (data-raw/benchmark/, inst/benchmark/) scores detectors human-labeled gold standard Serghiou et al. (2021) reports sensitivity, specificity, PPV, NPV accuracy bootstrap confidence intervals, alongside published Fig 2 numbers. Fixed .reroot_xml() handle bare <article> NCBI EFetch <pmc-articleset> roots. Previously returned empty document anything PMC OAI-PMH format, silently suppressed detection. Fixed unqualified str_detect()/regex() calls funding detector errored articles lacking structured funding statement.
